name: Total Daily Energy Expenditure Calculator
description: Industrial-grade TDEE calculator based on anthropometric inputs, activity factors, and metabolic adjustment formulas. Compliant with ISO 8996 (ergonomics of the thermal environment) and lean energy balance principles.

inputs.label: Age
inputs.businessContext: Age affects basal metabolic rate; used in Mifflin-St Jeor and Harris-Benedict equations.

businessContext_i18n.en: Age affects basal metabolic rate; used in Mifflin-St Jeor and Harris-Benedict equations.

inputs.label: Gender
inputs.businessContext: Gender-specific coefficients for BMR estimation per ISO 8996 and clinical standards.

businessContext_i18n.en: Gender-specific coefficients for BMR estimation per ISO 8996 and clinical standards.

inputs.label: Body Weight
inputs.businessContext: Primary driver of energy expenditure; used in all BMR formulas.

businessContext_i18n.en: Primary driver of energy expenditure; used in all BMR formulas.

inputs.label: Height
inputs.businessContext: Height influences lean body mass and BMR; used in Mifflin-St Jeor and Harris-Benedict.

businessContext_i18n.en: Height influences lean body mass and BMR; used in Mifflin-St Jeor and Harris-Benedict.

inputs.label: Activity Level
inputs.businessContext: Physical activity level (PAL) multiplier per WHO/FAO and ISO 8996 metabolic rate classification.

businessContext_i18n.en: Physical activity level (PAL) multiplier per WHO/FAO and ISO 8996 metabolic rate classification.

inputs.label: Body Fat Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Optional input for Katch-McArdle formula; improves accuracy for lean mass estimation.

businessContext_i18n.en: Optional input for Katch-McArdle formula; improves accuracy for lean mass estimation.

inputs.label: Athlete Mode
inputs.businessContext: Activates adjusted activity multipliers for high-frequency training (6+ sessions/week).

businessContext_i18n.en: Activates adjusted activity multipliers for high-frequency training (6+ sessions/week).

rules.message: Age must be between 10 and 120 years.
Weight must be between 20 and 300 kg.
Height must be between 50 and 250 cm.
Body fat percentage must be between 3% and 60%.
Athlete mode is incompatible with sedentary activity level.
Body fat percentage is recommended for Katch-McArdle formula accuracy.

thresholds.message: BMR is critically low; verify inputs.
TDEE exceeds typical range; consider professional assessment.
Very high energy expenditure may indicate overtraining risk.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
